meta.document: DJI Dealer Network Correlation — NASAO Impact Mapping
meta.description: Cross-references four major dealer networks against NASAO white paper state impact data. Maps DJI exposure, Trimble dependency, geographic coverage, and Forge commercial report GTM opportunity per network.
meta.key_thesis: Seiler, Frontier Precision, and Duncan Parnell are all large-format Trimble geospatial dealers whose UAS revenue is deeply tied to DJI — the same brand just grounded across their entire customer base. UVT is a pure-play public safety drone dealer that is DJI-heavy but already pivoting. All four represent high-concentration pockets of fleet transition pain that Forge commercial reporting can address.

trimble_dealer_context.why_it_matters: Trimble is the dominant platform in professional geospatial — survey, mapping, construction, GIS. The Trimble dealer network is how the majority of professional UAS gear was sold in the US for the past decade. These dealers built their UAS businesses by bundling DJI drones with Trimble workflows: RTK modules, Trimble Business Center processing, Pix4D, Terra — the complete stack. DJI was the drone that made Trimble field data collection more efficient and affordable. Now that stack is broken at the hardware layer.
trimble_dealer_context.trimble_uas_history: Trimble entered UAS directly via partnerships with Gatewing (acquired 2012), UX5 fixed-wing, then pivoted to a dealer-driven DJI integration model as DJI dominated the prosumer/enterprise market. Trimble dealers became de facto DJI integrators — the Matrice series with Trimble RTK modules was a standard workflow for survey firms.
trimble_dealer_context.revenue_dependency: For major Trimble dealers, UAS sales represent an estimated 15-35% of total revenue depending on the dealer's market mix. Survey, mapping, and construction firms — their core customers — are the exact audience most exposed to the DJI grounding via ASDA/FHWA federally-funded project restrictions.
trimble_dealer_context.dealer_pain_point: Trimble dealers now face: (1) their DJI inventory is stranded, (2) their customers can't use DJI on federal projects, (3) NDAA-compliant replacements cost 2-4x more and have limited availability, (4) they have no neutral guidance on what to recommend. This is the void Forge fills.
trimble_dealer_context.trimble_itself: Trimble has not released a branded NDAA-compliant drone. They are dependent on their dealer ecosystem to navigate the DJI transition. Dealers are largely on their own.

dealers.name: Seiler Instrument GeoDrones
dealers.trimble_relationship: Authorized dealer for Trimble, ESRI, ArcGIS, DJI, Bluebeam, and others. Long-standing Trimble relationship spanning decades — one of the key Midwest Trimble dealers.
dealers.dji_status: Authorized DJI dealer — still featuring DJI Matrice 400 as flagship product as of April 2026. Has NOT publicly pivoted to NDAA-compliant alternatives.
dealers.ndaa_pivot_status: None evident. Website and product catalog still DJI-forward.

wisconsin.notes: 100% fleet grounded — worst in NASAO survey. Seiler is primary dealer.

illinois.notes: Partial grounding, segregation by funding source

minnesota.notes: Minnesota not in Seiler primary footprint but adjacent

forge_opportunity.report_type: DJI Transition + Grant Eligibility — Midwest Edition
forge_opportunity.hook: Your Seiler DJI fleet is grounded for federal work. Here's what replaces it and what grants will pay for it.
forge_opportunity.gtm_note: Seiler has not pivoted — they are the incumbent dealer who sold customers into the problem. Forge is the neutral advisor Seiler should have been. Direct outreach to Seiler customer base is the highest-leverage Midwest play.

dealers.name: Frontier Precision
dealers.trimble_tier: Priority / Certified Service Center (9 Trimble Certified Service Centers)
dealers.trimble_relationship: One of the largest Trimble geospatial dealers in North America. 9 Trimble Certified Service Centers across ND, MN, CO, AK, HI, OR, WA, ID. Trimble Priority Support partner. Trimble Certified Trainers on staff. The Trimble relationship is the financial backbone of the company.
dealers.dji_status: Authorized DJI dealer — maintains DJI page on website, but has begun parallel NDAA pivot. Also carries Skydio, WISPR, Inspired Flight, ACSL SOTEN, Freefly, Quantum-Systems. Most advanced pivot of the three Trimble dealers.
dealers.ndaa_pivot_status: ACTIVE — carrying multiple NDAA-compliant platforms. Published NDAA transition guides. Partnered with Inspired Flight. Burgett NDAA alternatives guide published May 2025 — demonstrates they're advising customers on transition.

geographic_footprint.region: Northern Plains, Mountain West, Pacific Northwest, Southeast, National (UAS division)

oregon.notes: Frontier has certified service center in Portland/Tigard OR. Oregon is the white paper author — deep exposure.

minnesota.notes: Minneapolis is a major Frontier hub.

colorado.notes: 16 units grounded, 5 remain. Frontier has Arvada + Colorado Springs service centers.

idaho.notes: Idaho $15K→$42K replacement data point. Frontier has Twin Falls service center.

washington.notes: Partial grounding. Frontier has Seattle service center.

indiana.notes: Frontier Unmanned remote office in Kewanna IN.

illinois.notes: Frontier Unmanned remote offices in Congerville + East Peoria IL.

north_dakota.notes: HQ state — DOT not in NASAO sample but very likely exposed. Strong Frontier dealer presence.

georgia.notes: Frontier Unmanned has Georgia remote office.

dealers.unique_angle: Frontier's ag drone division (XAG, Hylio) is a separate business unit from their geospatial UAS — but both are DJI-adjacent. The FHWA restriction on federally-funded DOT projects hits Frontier's core customer base directly. Their North Dakota, Oregon, Colorado, and Minnesota DOT customers are all in the NASAO severe/moderate impact categories.

forge_opportunity.report_type: DJI Transition Report — Mountain West + Northern Plains Edition; DOT Fleet Replacement Guide
forge_opportunity.hook: Frontier's DOT customers in OR, CO, MN, ID are in the NASAO white paper by name. They need a replacement path, not just a product catalog.
forge_opportunity.gtm_note: Frontier is the most advanced in transitioning — they are NOT the problem here. But their customer base is still caught. The opportunity is not to replace Frontier but to be the intelligence layer that helps Frontier's customers navigate compliance, grants, and platform selection. Frontier may be a channel partner candidate.

dealers.name: Duncan-Parnell
dealers.trimble_tier: Exclusive Trimble dealer in multiple Southeast states — 8 Trimble Certified Service Centers. Trimble Priority Support partner.
dealers.trimble_relationship: Full-service Trimble Geospatial dealer since 1946. 75+ years with Trimble. Exclusive Trimble dealer in Tennessee. 8 Trimble Certified Service Centers across the Southeast. The Trimble relationship is existential to the business — Trimble gear is a majority of revenue.
dealers.dji_status: Authorized DJI dealer — active DJI product pages including Matrice 4E, Matrice 400, Mavic Enterprise. Also carries Wingtra, Inspired Flight, Parrot, Anzu Robotics, WISPR, Freefly, Quantum-Systems, YellowScan. Beginning pivot but DJI still prominently featured.
dealers.ndaa_pivot_status: PARTIAL — carrying some NDAA platforms (Inspired Flight, WISPR, Parrot, Freefly) alongside continued DJI. UAS team page shows public safety reps covering GA, FL, Carolinas, VA. Have a public safety drone division.

georgia.notes: Norcross GA branch. Georgia DOT 80% grounded, no replacement budget.

north_carolina.notes: NC not in NASAO sample — likely has significant exposure. Major DOT state with extensive federally-funded road/infrastructure projects. Duncan-Parnell HQ state — largest customer concentration.

virginia.notes: VA not in NASAO sample. Likely moderate exposure. Richmond VA branch.

south_carolina.notes: SC reported minimal impact — relatively good news for Duncan-Parnell's Charleston/Myrtle Beach customers.

tennessee.notes: TN reported minimal impact. Knoxville branch customers relatively insulated.

florida.notes: FL not in NASAO sample — but Florida passed state-level DJI ban in 2022 forcing $200M+ in grounding. Jacksonville + Orlando branches already faced this 2+ years ago. Customers there may already have transitioned or be stuck.

dealers.florida_special_note: Florida's 2022 state ban pre-dates the federal ASDA action. Duncan-Parnell's Jacksonville and Orlando customers were already grounded 2+ years ago with only $25M in state replacement funding for $200M+ in stranded assets. This is both a problem (stranded customers, lost UAS revenue) and an opportunity (those customers still need replacement guidance and grant funding they may not have secured).
dealers.unique_angle: Duncan-Parnell has a dedicated public safety UAS division with reps covering GA, FL, Carolinas, VA — the same states where DFR programs are active. Their public safety customers buying DJI Matrice M30T and M300 for LE use are directly exposed to ASDA restrictions on federal grant purchases.

forge_opportunity.report_type: DJI Transition Report — Southeast Edition; NC/GA DOT Fleet Guide; Florida Grant Recovery Report
forge_opportunity.hook: Georgia DOT: 80% grounded, no replacement budget. North Carolina: likely the same. Your Duncan-Parnell DJI fleet can't fly on federal projects. Here's the replacement path and the grants that pay for it.
forge_opportunity.florida_specific: Florida customers who haven't transitioned since the 2022 state ban still need NDAA-compliant replacements AND federal grant guidance they missed. Two-year-old problem, still unresolved for many agencies.
forge_opportunity.gtm_note: Duncan-Parnell's Southeast geography overlaps with multiple NASAO severe states (GA) and probable high-impact states (NC, VA). Their existing public safety UAS division creates a warm referral pathway for DFR reports.

dealers.name: Unmanned Vehicle Technologies (UVT)
dealers.ownership: Private — founded by Chris Fink (former 911 dispatcher, Rogers AR)
dealers.trimble_relationship: None — UVT is a pure-play UAS dealer, not a Trimble geospatial dealer. This is a fundamental structural difference. Their entire business is drones and robotics — they have no Trimble hardware revenue to fall back on.
dealers.dji_status: Tier I DJI Enterprise Dealer (direct purchase from DJI). Still carries DJI Matrice 4E, Matrice 4T, M30T actively. Also carries ANZU Robotics, Freefly Astro, Inspired Flight IF800/TOMCAT, Vantage Vesper, Wingtra Ray, Parrot ANAFI. DJI dock integration specialist.
dealers.ndaa_pivot_status: ACTIVE but selective — has an NDAA-compliant product page. Also maintaining DJI inventory. Serving agencies that still legally fly DJI (state-funded, not ASDA-restricted) while adding compliant alternatives for grant-funded procurement.

geographic_footprint.national_reach_notes: UVT serves 2,400+ government agencies and businesses nationwide — national dealer despite AR headquarters. Strong in South-Central and Mid-South. Regional reps in multiple states.

nebraska.notes: UVT active in Nebraska. 13 airframes impacted, ~$45K at risk.

arkansas.notes: UVT home state. Relatively low impact — 5 airframes, ~$100K. State has DJI restrictions passed 2023.

tennessee.notes: TN had minimal NASAO impact. UVT has presence.

iowa.notes: Iowa not in NASAO sample. UVT active here.

key_differentiators_vs_trimble_dealers: No Trimble dependency — pure drone focus means sharper UAS expertise but also higher risk if DJI revenue collapses
Founded by first responders — public safety DNA is genuine, not bolted on
Tier I DJI Enterprise — direct DJI relationship, which is now a liability and an asset (existing customers trust them, but transition is painful)
24/7 support model — 'Always On' brand promise means they have deeper operational relationships with agencies
TIPS Contract #240101 + Sourcewell Contract #011223-UNM — on cooperative purchasing vehicles, which simplifies LE procurement
DFR-specific program: UVT has its own DFR program page with FAA waiver support, site selection, SOPs — direct DFR competitor context for Forge
dealers.competitive_note: UVT is more of a potential channel partner than a competitor for Forge. They need neutral compliance intelligence (grant eligibility, NDAA matrix) that Forge produces. They have the agency relationships. Forge has the data layer. Partnership signal worth tracking.

forge_opportunity.report_type: DFR Full Intel + DJI Transition Report — South-Central Edition
forge_opportunity.hook: UVT's agency customers buying DJI for DFR are now blocked from grant-funded DJI procurement. Here's the NDAA-compliant replacement matrix and the grant path.
forge_opportunity.gtm_note: UVT is the most DFR-native of the four dealers. Their customers are the exact Forge DFR buyer. Either direct outreach to UVT agency customers OR a channel partnership with UVT (they distribute Forge reports to their 2,400+ agency customer base). Channel play is higher leverage.
forge_opportunity.channel_partnership_signal: UVT needs what Forge has: neutral NDAA compliance intelligence, grant eligibility matrices, FAA BEYOND pathway data. They have the relationships. Explore white-label or referral arrangement.

nasao_state_coverage_by_dealer.description: Which NASAO white paper states each dealer footprint covers

Oregon: Frontier Precision (Portland/Tigard service center, PDX region)
New York: None of the four — opportunity for East Coast dealer correlation
Colorado: Frontier Precision (Arvada + Colorado Springs service centers)

dji_revenue_exposure_ranking.most_exposed: Seiler — no pivot, full DJI catalog, Wisconsin 100% grounded customer base, no NDAA product guidance visible
dji_revenue_exposure_ranking.second: Duncan-Parnell — Florida already hit by 2022 state ban, Georgia now SEVERE, NC probable exposure, partial pivot
dji_revenue_exposure_ranking.third: Frontier Precision — most advanced pivot of the three Trimble dealers, but OR/CO/MN DOT customers are deep in NASAO impact categories
dji_revenue_exposure_ranking.least_exposed: UVT — most nimble, smallest Trimble dependency, already carrying NDAA alternatives, existing DFR program expertise

cross_network_analysis.trimble_dependency_note: Seiler, Frontier, and Duncan-Parnell's financial health depends heavily on Trimble hardware sales — survey equipment, GNSS receivers, data collectors, total stations. Trimble gear is recurring, sticky, and high-margin. Their UAS revenue was additive DJI sales that grew rapidly 2018-2024. The DJI grounding doesn't threaten their Trimble business directly, but it does: (1) erode UAS revenue that was 15-35% of total, (2) leave their UAS customers stranded without guidance, (3) damage trust with core survey/engineering clients who relied on dealer recommendations to buy DJI. Forge's commercial mapping reports address the trust damage by giving those customers a neutral path forward — something the dealer's DJI legacy makes it difficult for them to credibly provide.

combined_customer_base_estimate.overlap_note: Operators in overlapping states (IL, IN) may be customers of multiple dealers. Florida operators may have migrated dealers post-2022 ban.
combined_customer_base_estimate.total_addressable_forge_market: 15,000-50,000+ professional operators holding DJI fleets, all needing transition intelligence

under_covered: Texas (all four have some presence but thin)
geographic_gap_analysis.northeast_note: New York is SEVERE in NASAO (92% grounded, 23 airframes). None of the four dealers have primary Northeast presence. This is an uncovered market for Forge commercial reporting.

tier_1_immediate.reason: 100% grounded, no dealer pivot, no replacement budget — highest pain, lowest help
tier_1_immediate.forge_action: Wisconsin DJI Transition Report + COPS/HSGP grant guide. Target: Manitowoc County, Beaver Dam PD, Wisconsin DOT, county highway departments

tier_1_immediate.reason: 80% grounded, $225K at risk, no replacement budget. Norcross GA branch covers Atlanta metro
tier_1_immediate.forge_action: Georgia Fleet Transition Report. Target: Georgia DOT, Fulton/DeKalb/Gwinnett county agencies, survey firms on federally funded infrastructure projects

tier_1_immediate.reason: White paper author. 95% grounded, $250K+ at risk, no replacement budget. Frontier has Portland/Tigard service center
tier_1_immediate.forge_action: Oregon DOT Fleet Guide. Frontier may be channel partner candidate — they're already advising on transition, Forge provides the compliance intelligence layer

tier_2_follow.notes: 90% grounded, 16 units. Frontier has Arvada + Colorado Springs
85% grounded, $400K at risk. Seiler covers Indiana
Not in NASAO sample but probable high exposure. Duncan-Parnell HQ state. Major DOT infrastructure spend.
92% grounded — severe. No major dealer presence = highest Forge direct access opportunity

channel_partnership_candidates.rationale: Most advanced pivot, already advising customers on NDAA transition, has Inspired Flight partnership. Would benefit from Forge compliance intelligence layer. Non-competitive — Forge reports enhance their advisory value.
2,400+ agency relationships, DFR-native, already carrying NDAA platforms. Forge DFR reports are exactly what their customers need but UVT can't produce at scale. White-label or referral opportunity.
